name: charter
description: Know the project: gates substantive work on documented quality attributes (web projects get Lighthouse-aligned defaults — CWV, a11y, print CSS, progressive enhancement, components-by-default), maintains a committed Claude-facing roadmap/backlog file (cross-session/engineer coordination), keeps a project map (file→responsibility index) so a session orients from the map instead of re-scanning the tree, treats recorded decisions/ADRs as the alignment anchor (consult before reversing a choice; capture the evident ones when missing) so past choices aren't re-litigated, and records stack notes (languages/frameworks/versions) for modernization context. /charter:align reconciles open/proposed work against the recorded decisions + roadmap on demand (clean ≠ correct — surface drift and decision-contradictions before the work is done, not at review). Generates the docs from the codebase/git history when missing, reconciles them when present, and goes quiet once the policy is summarised in CLAUDE.md (claude-companion marker). Surfaces recently-merged commits next to the roadmap so reconciliation is concrete. Documents proportionally to complexity (baseline map + what's-next always; quality attributes for web; decisions/stack by judgment) as one compact brief, not a wall of per-doc prose. Read-only over the project (the hook detects; the model authors the files).
